name: GDPR data-export tables are z.record stubs
description: [FCR 2026-06-21 · L8-07] all export-table aliases are z.record(z.string(), z.unknown()); GET /account/export carries all personal data with no per-field contract validated before serialization. Fix: tighten each export-table alias to a real schema.
acceptanceCriteria:
Root cause: All 21 per-table export-row aliases in packages/schemas/src/account.ts (lines 30-50) are assigned to dataExportRowSchema (z.record(z.string(), z.unknown())), so GET /account/export serializes all personal data with no per-field contract. BUG-206 centralized the aliases but deferred the actual tightening. The canonical row types exist as Drizzle inferred types in @eduagent/database.

Variants: 21 table aliases (subjects, curricula, curriculumTopics, learningSessions, sessionEvents, sessionSummaries, retentionCards, assessments, xpLedger, streaks, notificationPreferences, learningModes, teachingPreferences, parkingLotItems, sessionEmbeddings, subscriptions, quotaPools, topUpCredits, needsDeepeningTopics, familyLinks, mentorActivityLedger), each currently accepting any shape. Tightening should be progressive — at minimum the highest-sensitivity tables (profiles/subscriptions/assessments) should get real schemas; the remainder should be documented with a tightening backlog if deferred.

Fix: for each alias, replace the dataExportRowSchema assignment with a z.object() schema whose fields match the corresponding Drizzle table column types, importing isoDateField and existing enums from the schema package where applicable. Update the per-table-alias identity assertions in packages/schemas/src/account.test.ts (line 124-125) to reflect the tightened schemas. Run: pnpm exec nx run schemas:test --no-coverage after each alias tightened.
riskImpact: No field/type contract on a GDPR personal-data export boundary.
